topic.what: Multi-turn jailbreaking via intention deception exploiting safe-completion vulnerabilities in frontier LLMs
topic.how: Disguises malicious intent as benign narrative (e.g., police report writing) and uses model consistency pressure to gradually extract harmful content across conversation turns
topic.why_matters: Reveals critical vulnerabilities in GPT-5 and Claude-Sonnet-4.5 safe-completion paradigm; introduces para-jailbreaking as new failure mode where model refuses direct harmful query but provides harmful alternatives

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of attacks eliciting harmful content from target model
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 experiments on 100 sampled AdvBench tasks
characteristics.context: Text-only setting; 63% total includes 12% direct + 51% para-jailbreaking

characteristics.what_it_measures: Success rate on sensitive domains (chemical, biological, nuclear, cybersecurity)
characteristics.context: High-stakes restricted topics under OpenAI safety policies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of successful attacks via harmful alternatives when direct refusal occurs
characteristics.context: Reveals novel para-jailbreaking failure mode unique to safe-completion models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success rate increase when benign images are added to text queries
characteristics.context: Images serve as entry points to reinforce benign surface intent

characteristics.what_itasures: Attack effectiveness with weaker attacker model (GPT-3.5-Turbo vs Qwen-Plus)
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 using GPT-3.5-Turbo as attacker model
characteristics.context: Demonstrates structural weakness independent of attacker strength

good_for: exploiting safe-completion vulnerabilities in frontier models
multi-turn conversation attacks requiring low technical skill
evaluating safety alignment robustness of LVLMs

novelty: Introduces para-jailbreaking: model refuses direct harmful query but provides harmful alternatives
Proposes intention deception as attack strategy leveraging model consistency pressure
Shows safe-completion paradigm introduces new vulnerability surface
Demonstrates vision modality amplifies jailbreaking success
open_problems: How to defend against para-jailbreaking specifically
Developing evaluation protocols for para-jailbreaking detection
Creating safeguards that reason about intent and consistency across turns
Understanding trust dynamics in multi-turn interactions for safety
